marketState: neutral, leaning bearish on storage surplus and uniformly bearish technicals, but capped by above-normal demand and a fairly-priced tape
summary: Seasonality data (the top-priority input) is entirely missing this run, so the medium-term read leans on weather, fair value, storage and COT alone. Storage is +184 Bcf above the 5-year average with a bearish +72 Bcf surprise build, and all six technical timeframes (5m-1w) show a bearish trend with a 50<200 EMA cross, which together argue for downside pressure. That is offset by cooling demand running +9.7% (1-7D) and +7.6% (8-16D) above the 5-year normal, and by price sitting almost exactly at modeled fair value at every horizon (front spread narrows from +$0.097 now to +$0.008 by +21D), leaving no clear mispricing to trade. Intraday RSI is already deeply oversold (15m 16, 1h 17.4), so chasing the bearish trend here offers poor risk/reward even though the trend itself is intact. Net, the evidence is mixed enough, and the seasonality gap large enough, to prefer waiting for a cleaner setup on both horizons.

longTerm.rationale: Seasonality (vsMedian, probHigher, typicalMove4w, reliability) is entirely null, removing the top-priority input for this horizon. The remaining evidence is mixed: storage is +184 Bcf above the 5-year average with a bearish +72 Bcf surprise build, but 8-16D demand is still +7.6% above normal and the fair-value gap shrinks from +$0.097 now to just +$0.008 by +21D, implying the market already expects convergence rather than a large directional move; managed money is net short but only at the 31st percentile of its 5-year range, so positioning isn't stretched.
longTerm.invalidation: A decisive break of front price outside the plus21D fair-value band ($2.506-$3.506), a sustained storage deficit turn vs the 5-year average, or seasonality data returning with a strong directional bias would invalidate this WAIT.
longTerm.trigger: Wait for seasonality metrics to repopulate and/or price to push outside the plus21D fair-value band with storage/COT confirmation before sizing a 3-12 week position.

shortTerm.rationale: Technicals are unanimously bearish (bearish trend, 50<200 EMA cross) across every timeframe from 5m to 1w, aligning with the bearish storage surprise, but 15m/1h RSI are already deeply oversold (16/17.4) with 4h/1d price below the lower Bollinger band, and front price sits right at modeled fair value (+$0.097 spread, status FAIR) with no mispricing edge to chase. Near-term weather demand is still +9.7% above the 5-year norm, arguing against piling into fresh shorts into an already-stretched tape.
shortTerm.invalidation: A clean RSI reset off oversold with a bullish MACD cross, or price breaking meaningfully above the plus7D fair-value band ($2.450-$3.450), would invalidate a bearish short-term bias.
shortTerm.trigger: Wait for either a bounce toward $3.10-$3.20 (prior resistance / futures-curve step toward Nov) to sell into, or an RSI reset off oversold followed by renewed downside momentum, before entering.

keyDrivers: Storage +184 Bcf vs 5-yr average with a bearish +72 Bcf build surprise vs forecast
Technicals unanimously bearish (trend + 50<200 EMA cross) across all six timeframes, 5m through 1w
Cooling demand +9.7% (1-7D) / +7.6% (8-16D) above 5-yr normal, though 24h trend is softening (-11)
Price essentially at modeled fair value at every horizon (spread narrows from +$0.097 now to +$0.008 by +21D)
Managed-money net short (-64,808) but only 31st percentile of 5-yr range - positioning not stretched
risks: Multiple intraday timeframes (15m RSI 16, 1h RSI 17.4, 4h RSI 23.4) are already oversold, raising bounce risk for fresh shorts
